candidates.name: Katie Hobbs
candidates.notes: Won 2022 governor's race by just 17,000 votes against election-denying Kari Lake. Refused to debate Lake during campaign. Social worker by training. As Secretary of State oversaw 2020 election — received death threats. Catholic. Running for reelection 2026 with 39% approval; faces Andy Biggs (Trump-endorsed) or Karrin Taylor Robson in general. Signed repeal of Arizona's 1864 near-total abortion ban.

profile.education: B.S. Social Work, Northern Arizona University; M.S.W. Arizona State University
profile.background: Social worker who ran a domestic violence shelter; served in Arizona Legislature (2011-2019) and as Secretary of State (2019-2023) before becoming 24th governor in 2023.

candidates.name: Mark Kelly
candidates.notes: Navy combat pilot flew 39 missions in Desert Storm. NASA astronaut — 4 shuttle missions, ISS commander. Twin brother Scott Kelly also astronaut (year-in-space mission). Married to Gabby Giffords, former congresswoman who survived 2011 assassination attempt — together they co-founded Giffords gun safety organization, the most prominent gun control group in the US. Catholic. Slammed Hegseth for Christian nationalism rhetoric in military. Term expires 2028. Both Arizona senators are now Democrats (Kelly + Gallego) — no Republican senator from Arizona as of 2026.

profile.education: B.S. Marine Engineering, U.S. Merchant Marine Academy (1986); M.S. Aeronautical Engineering, U.S. Naval Postgraduate School (1994)
profile.background: Retired Navy captain, NASA astronaut who flew four Space Shuttle missions. Gulf War combat pilot. Gun control advocate. Husband of former Rep. Gabby Giffords. Won 2020 special election.
profile.office: 516 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 2201 E. Camelback Rd, Suite 115, Camelback Plaza

candidates.name: Ruben Gallego
candidates.notes: Marine Corps Iraq War veteran (Lima Company 3/25 — suffered heavy casualties in Fallujah). Harvard graduate. First Latino senator from Arizona. First Colombian-American senator (alongside Bernie Moreno of Ohio). Heritage Action lifetime 5%. Entered politics as progressive but distanced himself from 'progressive' label for 2024 Senate run. Voted for Laken Riley Act — one of 12 Senate Democrats to do so. Previously married to Phoenix Mayor Kate Gallego. Defeated Kari Lake after Kyrsten Sinema chose not to run.

profile.background: Marine Corps Iraq War veteran. Former Arizona State Representative and U.S. House member. First Latino elected to represent Arizona in the U.S. Senate. Son of Colombian and Mexican immigrants.
profile.office: 302 Hart Senate Office Building
